feature: Home Page – Examples Listing
scenario: Display a list of links to all available example pages 
actions: Load the root URL 
expected: The page shall display a heading "Welcome to the-internet" and a subheading "Available Examples" The page shall present a vertical list of example names, each rendered as a hyperlink Each hyperlink text shall match the example name (e.g., "Checkboxes", "File Upload", "Typos") 

feature: Global Footer
scenario: Provide a consistent footer section attributing the site to Elemental Selenium on most example pages 
actions: Scroll to the bottom of the example page 
expected: The page shall display a footer containing the text "Powered by Elemental Selenium" 

feature: Rendering of Checkboxes
scenario: Display two checkboxes with labels 
actions: Load /checkboxes 
expected: The page shall display at least two checkbox input controls, labeled "checkbox 1" and "checkbox 2" or equivalent nearby text 

feature: Checkbox State Toggle
scenario: Allow the user to toggle checkbox states 
actions: Click a checkbox 
expected: On click, the target checkbox shall toggle its checked/unchecked state according t browser default behavior 

feature: Login Form Rendering
scenario: Display username and password fields with a Login button 
actions: Load /login 
expected: The page shall display a heading indicating it is a "Login Page" The page shall display an input field labeled "Username" The page shall display an input field labeled "Password" The page shall display a "Login" button 

feature: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
scenario: Grant access to a secure area when valid credentials are provided 
actions: Enter "tomsmith" in the Username field Enter "SuperSecretPassword!" in the Password field Click the "Login" button 
expected: The system shall authenticate the user as successful when the username is "tomsmith" and password is "SuperSecretPassword!" Upon success, the system shall navigate to a "secure area" page (e.g., via URL change /secure or equivalent) that indicates successful login 

feature: Error Handling for Invalid Credentials
scenario: Display error messages when login information is incorrect 
actions: Enter any username and/or password combination other than the known valid tomsmith / SuperSecretPassword! Click the "Login" button 
expected: The system shall display an error message indicating that the information is wrong The system shall not navigate to the secure area page 

feature: Dropdown Rendering
scenario: Display a selectable dropdown list with two options 
actions: Load /dropdown 
expected: The page shall display a descriptive heading "Dropdown List" The page shall present a dropdown (select element) with a default prompt "Please select an option" or equivalent text The dropdown shall include at least "Option 1" and "Option 2" as selectable options 

feature: Option Selection
scenario: Allow the user to change the dropdown selection 
actions: Click the dropdown and select an option (Option 1 or Option 2) 
expected: The dropdown's visible selection shall update to show the chosen option 

feature: Display of Asynchronous Controls
scenario: Provide an example page demonstrating asynchronous changes to elements such as checkboxes and input fields 
actions: Load /dynamic_controls 
expected: The page shall display descriptive text explaining that elements are changed asynchronously The page shall display at least one control (such as a checkbox or input field) that may be dynamically enabled, disabled, added, or removed after user actions 

feature: Dynamic Loading Examples Overview
scenario: Present two separate examples of dynamically loaded page elements 
actions: Load /dynamic_loading 
expected: The page shall display heading "Dynamically Loaded Page Elements" The page shall provide two example links or sections  Example 1: Element on page that is hidden  Example 2: Element rendered after the fact 

feature: File Uploader Rendering
scenario: Provide a file selection and upload capability 
actions: Load /upload 
expected: The page shall display heading "File Uploader" The page shall display a file input control allowing the user to choose a file from their system The page shall display an "Upload" button to initiate upload The page may present a drag-and-drop area for file selection per the descriptive text 

feature: Upload Action
scenario: Allow the user to upload a file and display confirmation 
actions: Click the "Upload" button 
expected: The system shall accept the selected file and navigate to or update the page t reflect that the file has been uploaded 

feature: Alerts Page Rendering
scenario: Present buttons that trigger different types of JavaScript dialogs 
actions: Load /javascript_alerts 
expected: The page shall display heading "JavaScript Alerts" The page shall display three clickable controls, labeled  "Click for JS Alert"  "Click for JS Confirm"  "Click for JS Prompt" The page shall display a "Result:" label where outcome text may appear 

feature: Basic Alert Trigger
scenario: Trigger a simple JavaScript alert 
actions: Click the "Click for JS Alert" button 
expected: The browser shall display a JavaScript alert dialog 

feature: Confirm and Prompt Triggers
scenario: Trigger a confirm and a prompt dialog respectively 
actions: Click "Click for JS Confirm" Click "Click for JS Prompt" 
expected: Clicking "Click for JS Confirm" shall display a JavaScript confirm dialog Clicking "Click for JS Prompt" shall display a JavaScript prompt dialog The "Result:" area may update to reflect user actions 

feature: Drag and Drop Example Rendering
scenario: Display a drag-and-drop area with draggable targets 
actions: Load /drag_and_drop 
expected: The page shall include a region that visually suggests drag-and-drop functionality 

feature: Data Tables Rendering
scenario: Display at least two table examples with user data 
actions: Load /tables 
expected: The page shall display heading "Data Tables" The page shall describe "Example 1" and "Example 2" Each example shall render a table with the following column headers  Last Name  First Name  Due  Web Site  Action Each table shall include at least the rows shown (Smith, Bach, Doe, Conway, etc.) with associated values 

feature: Row Actions (Edit/Delete) Representation
scenario: Provide "edit" and "delete" action controls in each row 
actions: View any table row in Example 1 or Example 2 
expected: Each row shall include an "edit" and a "delete" action in the Action column 

feature: Notification Message Rendering
scenario: Display a notification message above the heading 
actions: Load /notification_message_rendered 
expected: The page shall display heading "Notification Message" The page shall display a notification message above the heading, describing an utcome such as "Action successful" or "Action unsuccessful, please try again" 

feature: Load New Notification Message
scenario: Allow the user to load a new notification message 
actions: Click the link labeled "Click here to load a new message." 
expected: On each click, the system shall display a new notification message (text potentially different from previous message) 

feature: Entry Ad Display on First Load
scenario: Automatically display an advertisement modal when the page loads 
actions: Load /entry_ad 
expected: On page load, the system shall display an advertisement (modal or overlay) on the page 

feature: Close Ad and Prevent Reappearance
scenario: Allow the user to close the ad and suppress it on subsequent page loads 
actions: Click the "Close" control associated with the ad Reload or revisit /entry_ad 
expected: The ad shall be dismissed from view once "Close" is clicked On subsequent page loads, the ad shall not appear (as long as the setting remains active) 

feature: Re-enable Ad
scenario: Allow the user to re-enable the ad 
actions: Click the link or control labeled "click here" associated with "To re-enable it, click here." 
expected: After re-enabling, subsequent visits to /entry_ad shall again display the ad on page load 

feature: Random Typo Behavior
scenario: Randomly include or exclude a typo in the displayed text on page load 
actions: Load /typos Optionally refresh the page multiple times 
expected: The page shall display descriptive text explaining that a typo may appear randomly On some page loads, at least one word or punctuation shall be incorrect (e.g., "won,t") On other loads, the text may render without such typ 

feature: Add Element Button
scenario: Render a button for dynamically adding elements 
actions: Load /add_remove_elements/ 
expected: The page shall display heading "Add/Remove Elements" The page shall display a button labeled "Add Element" 

feature: Add Element Action
scenario: Add a new element to the page when the "Add Element" button is clicked 
actions: Click "Add Element" 
expected: On each click, the system shall add a new button or UI element (commonly "Delete") 

feature: Remove Added Elements
scenario: Allow removal of dynamically added elements 
actions: Click the control associated with each dynamically added element (commonly a Delete button) 
expected: The clicked element shall be removed from the DOM and no longer be visible 

feature: Element Disappearance/Reappearance Across Loads
scenario: On each page load, certain page elements may appear or disappear 
actions: Load the page multiple times (including refresh) 
expected: The page shall display descriptive text explaining elements may disappear/reappear n each load On some loads, certain menu items or elements shall be visible; on other loads, at least one of them shall not be present 

feature: Hover Targets Rendering
scenario: Display multiple user images with hidden details revealed on hover 
actions: Load /hovers 
expected: The page shall display a heading "Hovers" The page shall display at least three user images Each user image shall be associated with a set of hidden details  name: user1 (or user2, user3)  A link labeled "View profile" 

feature: Hover-to-Reveal Behavior
scenario: Reveal user details when hovering over each image 
actions: Move the mouse pointer over an image 
expected: While hovering over an image, the corresponding user's name and "View profile" link shall be visible When the mouse leaves the image area, the details may be hidden again 
